titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas Kirkpatrick to Margaret Huxley, 4 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Percy Kirkpatrick

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Thomas Percy Kirkpatrick (1869-1954) to Margaret Huxley (1856-1940) granting her use of the college's rooms for nursing examinations provided the dates don't clash with any of the college's own business.
From 1910 until 1954 Thomas Kirkpatrick served as the registrar for the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland. He also served as the general secretary of the Royal Academy of Medicine in Ireland. He took a particular interest in what were then termed venereal diseases; to encourage his patients (many of whom were prostitutes) to attend, he held a clinic for women at a discreet early morning hour to facilitate anonymity.
In 1915 Margaret Huxley took over the position as matron of the Dublin University Women’s Voluntary Aid Detachment’s Hospital. The VAD was a section of the Red Cross used to train civilian nurses to provide medical assistance during the war. The following year Huxley was tending to casualties of the Easter Rising as she opened her own private nursing home, on Lower Mount Street.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
4th FEBRUARY 16. Dear Miss Huxley, In reply to your letter of the 3rd inst., I have
the pleasure to inform you that the President and Fellows
have granted you the use of the College Hall for the Nursing
Examinations next March, provided always that the dates
days fixed for the Examinations do not clash with any
College business. I remain,
Yours faithfully,
Fellow & Registrar.
